% book-ko.tex — Pandoc XeLaTeX template for the Korean GEM-LLM book.
% Built on top of Pandoc 3.x default template variables. Only the
% Korean-/CJK-specific bits are customised; everything else falls back to
% Pandoc defaults via $$...$$ variables.
\documentclass[$if(fontsize)$$fontsize$,$endif$$if(papersize)$$papersize$paper,$endif$openany]{$if(documentclass)$$documentclass$$else$book$endif$}

% --- Page geometry --------------------------------------------------------
\usepackage{geometry}
$if(geometry)$
\geometry{$for(geometry)$$geometry$$sep$,$endfor$}
$else$
\geometry{a4paper,margin=2.5cm}
$endif$

% --- Fonts (XeLaTeX) ------------------------------------------------------
% NOTE: xeCJK omitted (not installed in this environment); Noto Sans KR via
% fontspec handles Korean glyphs adequately for proof-of-concept builds.
\usepackage{fontspec}
% Variable-axis Noto Sans KR confuses fontspec auto-discovery; pin to file.
\setmainfont{Noto Sans KR}[Path=/home/jovyan/.fonts/, UprightFont=NotoSansKR-Regular.ttf]
\setsansfont{Noto Sans KR}[Path=/home/jovyan/.fonts/, UprightFont=NotoSansKR-Regular.ttf]
\setmonofont{$if(monofont)$$monofont$$else$DejaVu Sans Mono$endif$}

% --- Locale & line-breaking ----------------------------------------------
% polyglossia korean requires xeCJK; fall back to english default.
\usepackage{polyglossia}
\setdefaultlanguage{english}
\XeTeXlinebreaklocale "ko"
\XeTeXlinebreakskip = 0pt plus 1pt minus 0.1pt

% --- Microtype, hyperlinks, graphics, captions, listings ------------------
\usepackage{microtype}
\usepackage{graphicx}
\usepackage{caption}
% Pandoc-emitted environments and macros require these:
\usepackage{amsmath}
\usepackage{amssymb}
\usepackage{longtable}
\usepackage{booktabs}
\usepackage{array}
\usepackage{calc}
\usepackage{etoolbox}
\usepackage{fancyvrb}
\usepackage{framed}
% Subset of Pandoc's default LaTeX preamble (admon, code, image floats).
\providecommand{\tightlist}{\setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}\setlength{\parskip}{0pt}}
\providecommand{\passthrough}[1]{#1}
\providecommand{\pandocbounded}[1]{#1}
\captionsetup{font=small,labelfont=bf}
\usepackage{listings}
\lstset{
  basicstyle=\ttfamily\small,
  breaklines=true,
  columns=fullflexible,
  keepspaces=true,
  showstringspaces=false,
  frame=single,
  framerule=0.4pt,
  rulecolor=\color{black!30},
}
\usepackage{xcolor}
\usepackage[dvipsnames]{xcolor}
\usepackage{hyperref}
\hypersetup{
  colorlinks=true,
  linkcolor=$if(linkcolor)$$linkcolor$$else$NavyBlue$endif$,
  urlcolor=$if(urlcolor)$$urlcolor$$else$NavyBlue$endif$,
  citecolor=$if(citecolor)$$citecolor$$else$NavyBlue$endif$,
  pdftitle={$title$},
  pdfauthor={$for(author)$$author$$sep$, $endfor$},
  pdflang={$lang$}
}

% --- TOC depth & numbering -----------------------------------------------
$if(numbersections)$
\setcounter{secnumdepth}{3}
$endif$

% --- User header-includes -------------------------------------------------
$for(header-includes)$
$header-includes$
$endfor$

\title{$title$$if(subtitle)$\\\large $subtitle$$endif$}
\author{$for(author)$$author$$sep$ \and $endfor$}
\date{$date$}

% Provide an abstract environment for `book` class (which lacks one by default).
\makeatletter
\@ifundefined{abstract}{
  \providecommand{\abstractname}{초록}
  \newenvironment{abstract}{\small\begin{quote}\noindent\textbf{\abstractname.}\quad}{\end{quote}\normalsize}
}{}
\makeatother

\begin{document}
\maketitle

$if(abstract)$
\begin{abstract}
$abstract$
\end{abstract}
$endif$

$if(toc)$
\tableofcontents
\clearpage
$endif$

$body$

\end{document}
